one_line_takeaway: MLLMs are far better at refusing explicitly malicious requests than at foreseeing the hazardous downstream consequences of benign-looking ones, and standard DPO-style preference alignment hits a capacity-scaling 'preference ceiling' that CASPO's dynamic, constitution-conditioned token-level self-distillation reward substantially outperforms on the paper's own OOD-MMSafe benchmark.
key_finding: Under Standard Mode, all evaluated MLLMs show substantial causal blindness: risk-appraisal zero-score rates (R0) range from 29.7% (Gemini-3-Pro, best closed-source) to 92.3% (LLaVA-1.5-7B, worst open-source); Qwen3-VL-4B fails on 67.5% of samples (this is the 'highest failure rate' figure cited in the abstract for closed-source high-capacity models region, though the raw text shows Qwen3-VL-4B at 67.5% is actually an open-source model -- the abstract's framing of 'high-capacity closed-source models' at 67.5% appears to refer to model-capacity trend generally rather than a single closed-source data point, which the paper's own Table 2 shows topping out near 61.5% for Doubao-1.6 among closed-source). Performance surges under Malicious Mode (e.g., GPT-5.1's RA jumps from 1.18/34.5% zero-rate to 1.96/1.5%), showing models detect explicit intent far better than latent consequence. Constitution Mode (safety-policy prompting) recovers much of this gap without training, e.g., InternVL-3.5-38B's R0 drops by 64.8 points and Qwen3VL-32B's by 33.4 points. DPO alignment on BeaverTails-V shows a 'preference ceiling': LLaVA-1.5-7B gains 38.9 points R0 reduction on Malicious-Mode intent detection but only 14.1 points on Standard-Mode causal projection, and Qwen3-VL-4B's Standard-Mode R0 actually worsens by -1.5 points after DPO, while safety-constitution prompting achieves +50.8 points of R0 improvement on the same model -- and POS analysis of top-KL-divergence tokens shows DPO-shifted tokens for Qwen3-VL are 56.5% punctuation vs. only 14.0% core content, evidencing a format-centric rather than semantic alignment shift. CASPO substantially closes this gap in training: on Qwen2.5-VL-7B, R0 falls from 82.6% (base) to 6.8% (TokR) / 7.3% (Hybrid), versus only 70.5% for plain BeaverTails-V DPO; on Qwen3-VL-4B, R0 falls from 67.5% (base) to 5.7% (Hybrid) versus 78.7% (worse than base) for plain BeaverTails-V DPO. CASPO variants also improve on external benchmarks SIUO (e.g., Qwen2.5-VL-7B Safe score rises from 34.33 to 88.02 with Hybrid) and MSS-Bench (Safe rises from 66.10 to 89.91), generally outperforming SPA-VL and plain BeaverTails-V DPO baselines while Effectiveness (E) scores are largely maintained or only slightly reduced.
core_contribution: The paper (1) formalizes a 'consequence-driven' MLLM safety paradigm, extending the standard token-level MDP alignment objective into a causal MDP where a causal-projection function maps a completed response to a terminal physical/social consequence state, and defines a Consequence-Driven Alignment (CDA) objective that rewards the terminal consequence rather than surface text; (2) introduces OOD-MMSafe, a benchmark of 455 curated query-image pairs across six safety domains (Violent Content, Self-Harm, Illegal Activity, Hate Speech, Privacy Violation, Sexual Content) built via a three-phase pipeline (Latent Hazard Synthesis using GPT-5/GPT-5 Mini/Doubao with SentenceTransformer dedup, Visual Context Grounding via Flux.2-dev/Qwen-Image plus manually crawled real images validated by Qwen3-VL-32B, and Causal Reasoning Refinement with human-in-the-loop filtering to remove undeclared-premise and speculative-hazard cases), evaluated with a tripartite Risk Appraisal (R) / Safety of Consequence (S) / Effectiveness (E) rubric scored 0-2 by an ensemble of GPT-5 and Qwen-3-VL-32B; (3) empirically demonstrates 'causal blindness' in 13 evaluated frontier MLLMs and a 'preference ceiling' in standard DPO-based alignment (gains shrink or go negative as model capacity grows, while token-level POS analysis shows DPO shifts become increasingly punctuation/format-centric for stronger models); and (4) proposes CASPO (Consequence-Aware Safety Policy Optimization), a policy-optimization algorithm that uses the log-probability discrepancy between a constitution-conditioned copy of the policy and the original policy as a dynamic, token-level self-distillation reward weight layered on top of outcome-based rewards, evaluated in OutR (outcome-only), TokR (token-only) and Hybrid variants.
threat_model: The paper's threat model is not adversarial/red-team in the jailbreak sense; queries are 'innocent-sounding precursors' (query benignness is an explicit curation criterion) that only become hazardous when combined with a specific visual context, and the benchmark explicitly excludes explicit malicious intent (that is tested separately as a 'Malicious Mode' ablation created by rewriting the same queries with overt malicious phrasing). The risk being modeled is that an MLLM, acting as an assistant to embodied/autonomous agents, fails to foresee the downstream physical or social consequence of an ostensibly benign response (e.g., advising on 'flight settings for close-up shots' near protected wildlife, or suggesting books that would let a toddler climb a shelf) -- i.e. a causal/consequence-blindness failure mode rather than a prompt-injection or explicit-harm-refusal failure mode.
limitations: The paper has no explicit 'Limitations' section (only an Impact Statement); the following are inferred directly from the paper's own methodology text, not stated as a self-critique by the authors. (1) Ground-truth scoring for all reported R/S/E numbers is produced by an LLM-judge ensemble (GPT-5 + Qwen-3-VL-32B average) rather than exclusively human raters, and no inter-annotator/judge-agreement statistic is reported in the main text. (2) The benchmark is comparatively small (455 samples across six top-level domains, with several sub-categories under 20 samples, e.g. Physical Tracking Risks n=9, Minor and Fetish Content n=9), which limits statistical granularity per sub-category. (3) Visual grounding mixes synthetic images (Flux.2-dev, Qwen-Image) with manually crawled real images; the paper validates synthetic image quality/semantic alignment using Qwen-3-VL-32B itself, which is also one of the two judge models, creating a potential shared-model bias between benchmark construction and evaluation. (4) The human-in-the-loop curation explicitly selects for 'cases where frontier models fail,' which by construction adversarially biases the benchmark toward known model weaknesses and may not reflect the natural base rate of consequence-blindness failures in deployment. (5) CASPO is only evaluated on three relatively small/mid-size open-source backbones (LLaVA-1.5-7B, Qwen2.5-VL-7B, Qwen3-VL-4B); no results are shown for training CASPO on larger or closed-source models, so it is untested whether the preference-ceiling problem CASPO targets persists or resolves differently at larger scale.

datasets_used: OOD-MMSafe (455 curated query-image pairs across 6 safety domains, introduced in this paper)
quantitative_results: Standard Mode R0 (risk-appraisal zero-score rate) on OOD-MMSafe: Gemini-3-Pro 29.7%, GPT-5.1 34.5%, Doubao-1.6 61.5%, Qwen3VL-32B 51.0%, Qwen3VL-4B 67.5%, InternVL-3.5-38B 82.4%, LLaVA-1.5-7B 92.3%
Malicious Mode RA jump for GPT-5.1: 1.18 -> 1.96 (R0 34.5% -> 1.5%)
Constitution Mode R0 reduction: InternVL-3.5-38B -64.8 points, Qwen3VL-32B -33.4 points

DPO on BeaverTails-V: LLaVA-1.5-7B Standard-Mode R0 gain 14.1 points vs Malicious-Mode gain 38.9 points; Qwen3-VL-4B Standard-Mode R0 gain -1.5 points (regression) vs safety-constitution prompting gain +50.8 points on same model
POS shift on DPO-aligned tokens for Qwen3-VL: punctuation 56.5% of top-shifted tokens vs core content 14.0%; for Safety Constitution: core content+modifiers 36.4% combined
CASPO on Qwen2.5-VL-7B (OOD-MMSafe): base RA 0.24/R0 82.6% -> CASPO Hybrid RA 1.80/R0 7.3% (vs BeaverTails-V DPO RA 0.41/R0 70.5%)
CASPO on Qwen3-VL-4B (OOD-MMSafe): base RA 0.58/R0 67.5% -> CASPO Hybrid RA 1.80/R0 5.7% (vs BeaverTails-V DPO RA 0.30/R0 78.7%, worse than base)
CASPO Hybrid on SIUO Safe score: Qwen2.5-VL-7B 34.33 -> 88.02 (+53.7); Qwen3-VL-4B 68.86 -> 89.82 (+21.0)
CASPO Hybrid on MSS-Bench Safe score: Qwen2.5-VL-7B 66.10 -> 89.91 (+23.8); Qwen3-VL-4B 77.31 -> 87.04 (+9.7)
baselines_compared: 13 off-the-shelf MLLMs evaluated zero-shot on OOD-MMSafe: Gemini-3-Pro-Think, Gemini-3-Pro, GPT-5.1, Doubao-1.6, o4-Mini, Qwen3VL-32B, Qwen3VL-8B, Qwen3VL-4B, InternVL-3.5-38B, InternVL-3.5-8B, Gemma-3-12B, QwenVL2.5-7B, LLaVA-1.5-7B
